---
title: "figure"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r fig-caterpillar-la}
#| label: fig-caterpillar-la
#| fig-cap: "Caterpillar plot of LA random intercepts (imputed full panel model, All Pupils). Top and bottom 10 LAs labelled; Brighton and Hove in pink."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 10

library(ggrepel)

# Extract LA random effects with conditional variances
re_list_imp <- ranef(imputed_models$all, condVar = TRUE)
re_la <- as.data.frame(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`)
re_la$group <- rownames(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`)

# Posterior standard errors
se_la <- sqrt(attr(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`, "postVar")[1, 1, ])
re_la$se <- se_la

re_la <- re_la %>%
  mutate(
    la_name = sub(":.*$", "", group),
    ci_lo = `(Intercept)` - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = `(Intercept)` + 1.96 * se,
    sig = ci_lo > 0 | ci_hi < 0,
    highlight = ifelse(grepl("Brighton", la_name), "Brighton and Hove", "Other")
  )

# Top and bottom 10 for labelling (+ always include Brighton)
extreme_la <- c(
  head(re_la$la_name[order(re_la$`(Intercept)`)], 10),
  tail(re_la$la_name[order(re_la$`(Intercept)`)], 10)
)
if (any(grepl("Brighton", re_la$la_name))) {
  extreme_la <- unique(c(extreme_la,
                          re_la$la_name[grepl("Brighton", re_la$la_name)]))
}

ggplot(re_la, aes(x = reorder(group, `(Intercept)`), y = `(Intercept)`)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi),
                width = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Map size to highlight and increase Brighton's size
  geom_point(aes(colour = highlight, size = highlight)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
#  geom_text_repel(
#    data = re_la %>% filter(la_name %in% extreme_la),
#    aes(label = la_name),
#    size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 25, force = 4, nudge_y = 0.005,
#    segment.colour = "grey60", segment.size = 0.3
#  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Other" = "#49a0c4",
                                  "Brighton and Hove" = "#e16fca"),
                      guide = "none") +
  # Adjust sizes specifically here
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Other" = 1.2, 
                                "Brighton and Hove" = 3.5),
                    guide = "none") +
  theme_pub +
  theme(
    # Remove axis text and ticks
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    # Remove axis labels (titles)
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    # Remove vertical grid lines
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-caterpillar-la
#| fig-cap: "Caterpillar plot of LA random intercepts (imputed full panel model, All Pupils). Top and bottom 10 LAs labelled; Brighton and Hove in pink."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 10

library(ggrepel)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Prepare Data
re_list_imp <- ranef(imputed_models$all, condVar = TRUE)
re_la <- as.data.frame(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`)
re_la$group <- rownames(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`)
se_la <- sqrt(attr(re_list_imp$`LANAME:gor_name`, "postVar")[1, 1, ])
re_la$se <- se_la

re_la <- re_la %>%
  mutate(
    la_name = sub(":.*$", "", group),
    ci_lo = `(Intercept)` - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = `(Intercept)` + 1.96 * se,
    sig = ci_lo > 0 | ci_hi < 0,
    highlight = ifelse(grepl("Brighton", la_name), "Brighton and Hove", "Other")
  )

# 2. Create Plot Object
p <- ggplot(re_la, aes(x = reorder(group, `(Intercept)`), y = `(Intercept)`)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi),
                width = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = highlight, size = highlight)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Other" = "#49a0c4",
                                  "Brighton and Hove" = "#e16fca"),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Other" = 1.2, 
                                "Brighton and Hove" = 3.5),
                    guide = "none") +
  theme_pub +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  )

# 3. Print to screen (optional)
print(p)

# 4. Save High-Res
ggsave(
  filename = "la_caterpillar_highres.png", 
  plot = p,               # Explicitly use the object 'p' to avoid plotly errors
  width = 10, 
  height = 8, 
  dpi = 600, 
  bg = "white"
)
```
